title: alyssum calycinum var. sublineare
intro: Alyssum calycinum var. sublineare, also known as a type of creeping alyssum, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant with delicate white flowers that blooms in the summer months. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits of Alyssum calycinum var. sublineare include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Alyssum calycinum var. sublineare include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically made from the leaves and flowers of the plant.
